stem: A public health study surveys 25 residents surveyed and observes 5 positive outcomes out of 25 (20.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (8 observations, rate = 12.5%) and Group B (17 observations, rate = 23.5%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 33% and 50% of the population. Estimate the true vaccination coverage and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.2037 (range [0.1976, 0.2105]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0886, 0.3913]. Point estimate: 0.2037.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.42: 0.42×0.16 + 0.58×0.2353 = 0.2037.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
